data.title: Juneyao Airlines' Passenger Capacity Rises 19% in January
data.description:
Juneyao Airlines' passenger capacity jumped 19% year on year to about 5.39 billion available seat kilometers in January, according to a Tuesday filing with the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

Total passenger count grew 6.5% to 2.4 million during the month.

Cargo and mail capacity increased 34% to 172.3 million available freight-tonne kilometers.

Total cargo and mail carried rose 27% to 14,305.2 tons, the filing said.

Shares slipped 2% during Tuesday's afternoon trading.

data.title: UK Wages Rise as Expected
data.description:
Regular pay in the UK, excluding bonuses, increased by 5.9% year-on-year to GBP 664/week in the three months to December 2024, accelerating from 5.6% in the previous period and matching market consensus.

It was the fastest rise in eight months, with wage growth quickening in both the private sector (6.2% vs. 5.9%) and the public sector (4.7% vs. 4.2%).

Wholesale, retailing, hotels, and restaurants saw the most significant increase (6.6%), followed by construction (6.5%), manufacturing (6%), services (6%), and finance and business services (5.9%).

Adjusted for inflation, wage growth in real terms rose by 2.5%, the highest in seven months, up from the prior figure of 2.4%.

data.title: IMotion Automotive Technology Raises HK$228 Million via Share Placement
data.description:
IMotion Automotive Technology (Suzhou) raised HK$228.4 million in net proceeds by placing 1,190,200 shares to at least six investors at HK$20.88 apiece, a Monday filing with Hong Kong Exchange said.

The shares represent 4.63% of the Chinese autonomous driving solution company's enlarged issued share capital.

The company will use the proceeds from the issue to boost its R&D of certain solutions and products, to expand its overseas sales and service network, and for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: The Hour Glass Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
The Hour Glass bought back 5,600 shares in the open market on Monday for SG$8,784 or SG$1.56 apiece, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The luxury watch retail group has been authorized to buy back 64.8 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 518,300 shares.

data.title: UK Unemployment Rate Steady at 4.4% in Quarter to December 2024
data.description:
Britain's unemployment rate came in at 4.4% in the quarter to December 2024, unchanged from the prior three-month period, the Office for National Statistics said Tuesday.

The latest reading is below the consensus estimate of 4.5%.

Meanwhile, the UK employment rate came in at 74.9%.

data.title: Ad-Manum Finance's CFO Resigns
data.description: Ad-Manum Finance said its chief financial officer, Vikas Gupta, has resigned from the company, effective Feb. 15, according to a Tuesday filing to the BSE.

data.title: IIFL Finance to Raise Up to INR1 Billion Via Debt Offering; Shares Down 4%
data.description:
IIFL Finance has received board approval to raise up to 1 billion Indian rupees through the allotment of non-convertible debentures on private placement basis, according to a Tuesday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

The company will allot 100 unsecured, redeemable, non-convertible, subordinated debentures of the face value of 10 million rupees each.

The debentures will carry a coupon rate of 9.5% per annum and will mature on Oct. 16, 2034. They will be listed on the National Stock Exchange of India.

Shares of the company were down 4% in recent trade.

data.title: UK Claimant Count Jumps in January
data.description:
The number of people claiming unemployment-related benefits in Britain rose by 22,000 in January, against the revised decrease of 15,100 in the prior month, according to data from the Office for National Statistics published Tuesday.

Analysts expected a 10,000 increase for the month.

data.title: UK Average Earnings Including Bonuses Up 6% in Q4 2024
data.description:
Britain's average weekly pay, including bonuses, grew 6% year over year in the quarter to December 2024, compared with the revised 5.5% rise in the prior three-month period, the Office for National Statistics said Tuesday.

Analysts expected a 5.9% increase for the period.

Excluding bonuses, the growth in average weekly earnings was 5.9% in the fourth quarter of 2024, consistent with the consensus estimate and against the 5.6% earlier.

data.title: Sweden Inflation Rate Revised Slightly Lower
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Sweden rose to 0.9% in January 2025, slightly lower than the preliminary estimate of 1%, but edging up from a four-year low of 0.8% in December 2024.

Despite the uptick, this still marked the sixth consecutive month that the inflation rate was below the Riksbank's 2% target.

Prices accelerated for food and non-alcoholic beverages (2.17% vs. 1.73% in December), clothing and footwear (3.72% vs. 2.09%), recreation and culture (1.25% vs. 0.63%), education (4.24% vs. 1.92%), and restaurants and hotels (3.7% vs. 3.04%).

In addition, prices rebounded for transport (0.41% vs. -0.5%).

Meanwhile, costs moderated for alcoholic beverages and tobacco (1.06% vs. 2.88%), health (3.91% vs. 4.51%), and miscellaneous goods and services (3.68% vs. 3.75%).

Moreover, deflation continued for housing and utilities (-0.86% vs. -0.37%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices stalled in January 2025, remaining unchanged from the preceding month.

data.title: TT International's Attributable Loss Balloons in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
TT International's attributable loss to owners widened to SG$1.3 million in the fiscal quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, from SG$260,000 a year earlier.

Revenue declined 27% year over year to SG$4.5 million from 6.2 million, due to a decrease in sales and gross profit, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange.

data.title: KKR to Boost Stake in Eni Unit Enilive to 30%
data.description:
KKR agreed to increase its stake in Italian energy company Eni's (ENI.MI) mobility decarbonization unit Enilive to 30%.

The private equity giant agreed to acquire another 5% shareholding in Enilive for 587.5 million euros, according to a Tuesday release. The acquisition remains subject to regulatory approvals.

The transaction is in line with Eni's agreement with KKR covering a 25% Enilive stake, which is expected to close by March.

data.title: MTQ Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
MTQ bought back 15,400 shares in the open market on Monday for SG$4,264 or SG$0.275 apiece, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The company has been mandated to repurchase about 22.5 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 186,200 shares.

data.title: Rising Eurozone Bond Yields Signal Prospects of Higher Defense Spending — Market Talk
data.description: Eurozone government bonds experienced some weakness on Monday, with yields rising as markets price in U.S.-EU relations souring, according to Pepperstone's Michael Brown in a note. Bond markets are also pricing in a risk premium associated with the upcoming German elections, the senior research strategist says. Higher government bond yields also signal the potential of higher investor insurance as European nations attempt to finance a sharp rise in defense spending, Brown says. The eurozone 10-year bond yields rose by 4 to 7.5 basis points across the board on Monday, according to Tradeweb data. Eurozone bond investors will be closely watching for the release of Germany's ZEW sentiment survey on Tuesday, while bond issuance will come from Germany and Finland. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Bitcoin Miner HIVE Names Former Apple Exec to Lead Cloud Unit in $100 Million Push
data.description:
HIVE Digital Technologies, the publicly listed Bitcoin (BTC) miner (NASDAQ: HIVE) has announced the appointment of Craig Tavares as President and Chief Operating Officer of its Buzz HPC division, which focuses on GPU Cloud and High-Performance Computing (HPC) services

HIVE Taps Ex-Apple Executive for Cloud Computing Expansion

The appointment comes as HIVE, a sustainable blockchain and AI infrastructure provider, aims to scale its HPC business to $100 million in revenue. Tavares brings more than two decades of experience in digital infrastructure and cloud services to the role.Frank Holmes, Executive Chairman of HIVE

"With his unique skillset of leadership, entrepreneurialism, and ethics, alongside his explicit and tacit knowledge of digital infrastructure, cloud, and high-performance computing, he is a true champion of our vision," said Frank Holmes, Co-Founder and Executive Chairman of Buzz HPC, a HIVE Digital Technologies company.

Tavares's career spans leadership roles at major technology companies, including Apple, where he led product and program strategy for third-party clouds and data centers. His experience includes launching a global cloud business at Aptum and establishing partnerships with industry giants Microsoft and Amazon Web Services.

The decision follows the Wall Street-listed Bitcoin mining company's fourth-quarter earnings report, which showed $29.2 million in revenue and an adjusted EBITDA of $17.3 million. Although these figures are slightly lower than the previous year’s results, the company has increased its Bitcoin reserves, now holding 2,805 tokens worth approximately $260.8 million.

AI and HPC Move

The new executive's appointment aligns with HIVE's strategy to strengthen its position in the rapidly evolving cloud computing market. The company currently operates a sovereign AI GPU platform powered by renewable energy, which it plans to expand under Tavares's leadership.

"Buzz already provides one of the industry's highest-performance Sovereign AI GPU platforms," Tavares said. "We will expand it through vertically integrated infrastructure and a suite of differentiated HPC service offerings."

HIVE Digital Technologies
@HIVEDigitalTech

HIVE is excited to announce the appointment of Craig Tavares as President and Chief Operating Officer of Buzz HPC, where he will lead the GPU Cloud and High-Performance Computing (HPC) data center business. ⬇️ pic.twitter.com/LrYeSBFQ8k

Feb 18, 2025

According to the recent analysis from VanEck, the HPC move seen in the Bitcoin mining industry may unlock almost $40 billion in value for publicly traded miners.

In conjunction with the appointment, HIVE announced the granting of 1,090,000 Restricted Share Units (RSU) to select employees, officers, directors, and consultants, including Tavares. The RSUs are subject to a one-year vesting period under TSX Venture Exchange regulations.

HIVE Digital Technologies also continues to expand its operations across Canada, Sweden, and soon Paraguay, planning to expand its hash rate to 25 EH/s by September.

data.title: Anglo American Sells Nickel Business for Up to $500 Million
data.description:
(07:00 GMT) *Anglo Amer Agrees Sale of Nickel Business
(07:01 GMT) *Anglo American to Sell Nickel Business for up to $500M
(07:05 GMT) *Anglo American to Sell Nickel Business to MMG Singapore Resources
(07:06 GMT) *Anglo American: Consideration Includes Upfront Cash Consideration of $350M
(07:06 GMT) *Anglo American: Deal Has Potential for Up to $100M in Price-Linked Earnout
(07:06 GMT) *Anglo American: Sale Important Milestone Towards Simplifying Portfolio
(07:14 GMT) Anglo American Sells Nickel Business for Up to $500 Million

By Michael Susin

Anglo American said it agreed to sell its nickel business to a subsidiary of Chinese government-backed mining company MMG for up to $500 million in cash.

The London-listed miner said Tuesday that the deal comprises an upfront cash payment of $350 million at completion, with the remaining consideration subject to certain milestones and investment decisions.

Anglo's nickel business comprises two ferronickel operations and two growth projects in Brazil.

"The sale of our nickel business after a highly competitive process marks a further important milestone towards simplifying our portfolio to create a more highly valued copper, premium iron ore, and crop nutrients business," Chief Executive Duncan Wanblad said.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: SIA Engineering Buys Back More Shares
data.description:
SIA Engineering repurchased 140,000 shares in the open market on Monday for SG$335,464, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The engineering services company has been authorized to buy back 22.5 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 5.2 million shares.

data.title: Vaisala's Board of Directors resolved on share-based long-term incentive plans
TTF Gas Hits 4-week Low
data.description:
TTF Gas decreased to a 4-week low of 47.42 EUR/MWh.

Over the past 4 weeks, Natural Gas EU Dutch TTF gained 0.03%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 101.38%.

data.title: UK Gas Hits 4-week Low
data.description:
UK Gas decreased to a 4-week low of 114.46 GBp/thm.

Over the past 4 weeks, Natural Gas UK GBP lost 5.35%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 97.59%.

data.title: Tokyo Most Actives
The Shangai Composite Index Closes 0.91% Lower
data.description:
In China, the Shangai Composite Index fell 31 points or 0.91 percent on Tuesday.

Leading the losses are China Fortune (-7.61%), Yonghui Superstore (-5.79%) and Shanghai Electric (-4.53%).

Top gainers were Bank Of China (2.02%), Shanghai Pudong (1.95%) and ICBC (1.87%).

data.title: Guangdong Haomei New Materials Plans Joint Venture in Morocco
data.description:
Guangdong Haomei New Materials plans a joint venture with Lingyun Industrial to establish a production base in Tangier, Morocco, according to a Tuesday filing with the Shenzhen Bourse.

The JV, with a registered capital of 100 million yuan, will manufacture battery casings, body structures, and high-strength roll-formed components for European and North African markets.

Haomei will hold 49% equity via a Hong Kong subsidiary, while Lingyun will hold 51%. This strategic move aims to localize supply, enhance competitiveness, and expand into European markets.

data.title: Antofagasta: 2024 Capex Growth Driven by $285.7M Centinela Project
Enlitic Partners with US-Based Medical Technology Provider
data.description:
Enlitic has partnered with GE HealthCare to enhance artificial intelligence-driven migrations for Enterprise Imaging solutions, according to a Monday filing with the Australian bourse.

Building on an existing agreement with GE HealthCare's unit, Laitek, the partnership aims to streamline application migrations, improving diagnostic and care capabilities for healthcare providers, the filing said.

The collaboration will leverage AI-powered migration tools to optimize imaging data quality and ensure interoperability across platforms, the filing added.

Both parties are finalizing the specific details of the deal, per the filing.

Enlitic's shares surged 47% on market close.

data.title: UK Jobless Rate Steady at 4.4%
data.description:
The United Kingdom’s unemployment rate held steady at 4.4% from October to December 2024, defying expectations of a rise to 4.5%.

This remains the highest level since the three months ending in May, driven by an increase in individuals unemployed for up to 12 months.

Compared to the same period last year, the number of people unemployed for both up to 12 months and over 12 months increased.

data.title: Gentoo Media reports Q4 2024
data.description:
Gentoo Media reports Q4 2024

PR Newswire

ST JULIANS, Malta, Feb. 18, 2025

Gentoo Media closes the year with another record-breaking quarter, marking the 16th consecutive period of all-time high revenue for the company.

ST JULIANS, Malta, Feb. 18, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Gentoo Media Inc. presents its Q4 2024 financial report, with record-high revenue of EUR 35.9 million, up 38% year-over-year, marking the 16th consecutive quarter of growth. Full year revenue was EUR 124.5 million (an increase of 41% YoY) with EBITDA of EUR 56.6 million representing a margin of 45%. (excluding split-related costs).

2024 was a transformative period for the company and after successfully completing a strategic split, the Group is well positioned as a stand-alone business to capitalise on growth opportunities and enhance its market presence.

Jonas Warrer, CEO of Gentoo Media: "I am pleased to present our fourth quarter report for 2024, marking the 16th consecutive period of all-time high revenue for Gentoo Media. This sustained growth demonstrates the strength of our strategy and commitment to delivering long-term value to our shareholders".

Mikael Harstad, Chairman of the Board at Gentoo Media:"Gentoo Media has established itself as a major player in a global industry but still only has a small market share, presenting substantial growth opportunities. Management and the board are excited about the road ahead, further positioning the company as an industry leader in 2025 and beyond."

Q4 2024 highlights

  • Gentoo Media reported all-time high revenues of EUR 35.9 million, up 38% YoY, of which 18% organic growth
  • EBITDA before special items reached EUR 14.3 million, an EBITDA-margin of 40%
  • EBIT reached EUR 9.8 million, with a margin of 27%
  • Quarterly value of deposits reached EUR 200 million, up 27% YoY, bringing full-year deposits to EUR 767 million
  • By Q4 Gentoo Media is now purely an affiliate-focused business after distribution of the Platform & Sportsbook division to shareholders on 30 September 2024
  • Post-split, cash flow from operations will improve, enhancing capital allocation flexibility. Gentoo Media is now better positioned to drive strong returns and investor value
  • Throughout 2024 Gentoo Media has funded Platform & Sportsbook business with EUR 46 million

Investor presentation and webcast

CEO of Gentoo Media, Jonas Warrer, will host a presentation of the Q4 2024 results via livestream at 10:00 CET today. The presentation will be followed by a Q&A-session, and investors, analysts and journalists are welcome to participate. The presentation will be given in English.

Link to the livestream:

https://www.redeye.se/events/1067755/live-q-gentoo-media-2

For further information, contact:

Jonas Warrer, CEO, jonas.warrer@g2m.com, +45 30788450

Mikael Harstad, Chairman of the Board, mikael.harstad@g2m, +44 737674852

Tore Formo, Group CFO, tore.formo@g2m.com +47 91668678

This information is information that Gentoo Media Inc. is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the contact person set out above, at 08:00 CET on 18 February 2025.

About Gentoo Media

Gentoo Media is a market-leading affiliate connecting operators and players in the online gambling and sports betting industry. Gentoo Media offers an array of iGaming affiliate solutions, such as paid marketing expertise and quality traffic through our prominent industry sites including AskGamblers, Time2Play, CasinoTopsOnline, WSN and Casinomeister. Through its subsidiary Titan Inc. the company also sells SEO and content services to online businesses. In 2024, Gentoo Media (formerly GiG Media) became Gentoo Media Inc. following a legal split separating the Media and Platform and Sportsbook business in Gaming Innovation Group (GiG) into two independently listed companies. Gentoo Media Inc. is dual listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange (ticker "G2MNO") and Nasdaq Stockholm (ticker "G2M"). www.gentoomedia.com

This information was brought to you by Cision http://news.cision.com

https://news.cision.com/gentoo-media-inc/r/gentoo-media-reports-q4-2024,c4107042

data.title: KOSPI Rallies for 6th Day
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI rose 0.6% to 2,627 on Tuesday, marking its sixth straight gain and a three-month high, as optimism grew in China, South Korea’s largest trading partner, after President Xi Jinping’s meeting with top business leaders to support the private sector.

At home, South Korea announced plans to secure 10,000 high-performance GPUs this year to stay competitive in the global AI race.

The government also unveiled a record 360 trillion won ($249.3 billion) in trade financing to support exporters, helping businesses navigate intensifying US tariff policies, further boosting sentiment in trade-dependent sectors.

Among key stock indexes, chip giant Samsung Electronics jumped 1.6% after canceling 3.05 trillion won worth of previously repurchased shares.

Additionally, Hanwha Aerospace soared 11.4% after showcasing its long-range surface-to-air missile system at a defense exhibition in Abu Dhabi.

Other notable gainers included Hyundai Heavy Industries (1.5%) and KB Financial (1.4%).

data.title: European Markets Set for Positive Open
data.description:
European equity markets were poised to open slightly higher on Tuesday as investors closely watched geopolitical developments, with US officials set to meet their Russian counterparts this week to discuss ending the war in Ukraine.

Meanwhile, European leaders held an emergency summit in Paris on Monday to address their exclusion from peace negotiations, agreeing to boost defense spending.

Investors will also be looking at French inflation data, along with economic sentiment indicators for the Eurozone and Germany.

On the corporate front, earnings reports are expected from Capgemini and InterContinental Hotels Group.

Euro Stoxx 50 and Stoxx 600 futures traded flat to slightly positive in premarket activity.

data.title: Gabungan AQRS Unit Files Case Over Losses Linked to SUKE-CA3 Project
data.description:
Gabungan AQRS unit Gabungan Strategik and Syarikat Muhibah Perniagaan dan Pembinaan filed a lawsuit at the High Court of Malaysia against Turnpike Synergy and four other defendants.

The claim involves losses and damages related to the SUKE-CA3 project, according to a Monday Malaysian bourse filing.

The litigation is ongoing and the company expects no immediate financial impact, it said.
